entities.title: Zergvert waitlist (Phase B — ready when 1 surface lives)
Blog editing tool — Obsidian, CMS, or Google-docs-style collab?
entities.question:
Tasks/inbox #29 (zb:WEB-44) flags an open call on the blog content editing
workflow. Today Matt writes in Obsidian → ships to `~/zerg/web/src/public/content/blog/`
via a manual move + Nuxt build. With Idan also publishing technical pieces and
the 8-week content cadence ramping (`Writing/Topic Queue.md`), the bottleneck
is moving to (a) review velocity (when Idan + Matt both touch a piece), and
(b) external contributor ergonomics (if a case-study customer needs to edit).
Three real options: stay on Obsidian-first, add a CMS (Webflow/Sanity/Strapi),
or use Google-docs-style collab (Notion/Coda/Google Docs) as the drafting layer.
entities.context:
Memory note feedback_blog_author_routing.md routes pieces between Matt + Idan
based on type. Memory feedback_internal_review_pack_format.md and the
fakematt-copyedit skill expect markdown files as input. Any new CMS would
need round-trip with markdown OR a custom export script.

options.label: Stay Obsidian-first; ship via Nuxt build
options.implications:
No new tool. Voice/style discipline preserved (fakematt-copyedit anchors).
Friction stays on multi-author review + external contributors. Best fit
if cadence is 1-2 pieces/week and contributors are small.

options.label: Add a CMS (Webflow / Sanity / Strapi)
options.implications:
Better external contributor UX (web-based editor). Marketing team can
schedule + edit without CLI access. Cost: new tool to maintain, markdown
export pipeline, brand-styling discipline split across two systems.
Multi-week setup.

options.label: Use Google Docs (or Notion/Coda) as the drafting+review layer
options.implications:
Drafts live in Docs (great for Idan↔Matt async review + customer signoff
loops on case studies). Final piece exported as markdown → Obsidian →
Nuxt. Lower friction than CMS, but markdown round-trip is lossy
(especially for callouts, embedded media).

unblocks: Andesite / CesiumAstro / Durable case study signoff workflows
Future hires writing on zergai.com without CLI access

entities.title: Bundle as lead motion or in-product upgrade nudge only Phase 1-2?
entities.question:
Should the Zstack Bundle ($19/seat for the suite) be the LEAD marketing
motion in Phase 1-2 — hero pricing, primary CTA on zergai.com,
comparison-page anchor — OR should we lead with individual microproducts
and treat the Bundle as a Pro-tier in-product upgrade nudge only?
entities.context:
Sourced from Zergboard MAR-33 ([Decision] high priority). Memory note
project_zstack_pricing.md describes the vampire-attack pricing model:
free / $1 / $9 / $19 / Enterprise per microproduct. The Bundle is a
cross-product upsell. Leading with it positions Zstack as a suite (Linear
competitor) vs leading with microproducts positions us as best-in-class
individual tools (Notion+Calendly+Otter+Front competitor each). Has
downstream effects on every comparison page, the homepage hero, and the
Phase 2 launch sequencing.

options.label: Lead with Bundle in Phase 1-2 marketing
options.implications:
Homepage hero anchors on $19 Bundle. Comparison pages benchmark Bundle
vs competitor stack-cost. Best-suite-cheapest narrative. Risk: single
microproducts feel like an afterthought; individual ICPs (PM-only,
ops-only) get a confusing pitch.

options.label: Lead with individual microproducts; Bundle = in-product upgrade nudge
options.implications:
Each microproduct gets its own positioning + landing page. Bundle CTA
surfaces inside-product (e.g., "add ZergCal to your workspace for $9").
Cleaner ICP per surface. Risk: dilutes the cross-product moat;
competitors can pick off one microproduct at a time.

options.label: Hybrid — Bundle on homepage, microproducts on each /product page
options.implications:
Homepage hero = Bundle for suite-buyers. Per-product pages = standalone
positioning. CTA on microproduct pages: "or save 60% with the Bundle."
More marketing surface area to maintain; the Bundle vs single split is
explicit on every page.

unblocks: Phase 2 launch sequencing (bundle launch vs per-product launches)

cascade.bundle-lead: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — DECIDED bundle-lead per dec-bundle-lead-motion. Homepage hero anchors on $19 Bundle. All comparison pages benchmark Bundle vs competitor stack-cost. Best-suite-cheapest narrative.'}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-zergboard-launch.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — Bundle-lead motion decided. Zergboard launch copy should reference the Bundle CTA, not stand-alone Zergboard pricing.'}}]
cascade.microproducts-lead: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — DECIDED microproducts-lead per dec-bundle-lead-motion. Each microproduct gets its own positioning. Bundle CTA surfaces in-product only. Cleaner ICP per surface.'}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-zergboard-launch.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Microproducts-lead motion. Zergboard launch is standalone; Bundle isn't the hero of this announcement.\"}}]
cascade.hybrid: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — DECIDED hybrid per dec-bundle-lead-motion. Homepage hero = Bundle. Per-product pages = standalone positioning + 'save with Bundle' CTA. More surface area to maintain.\"}}]

entities.title: GitHub account for Zerg work — personal or matteisn-zerg?
entities.question:
Tasks/inbox #28 (zb:ZER-210) flags an open call on which GitHub account is
primary for Zerg work. Currently a mix — some PRs land under `matteisn`, some
under `matthew-zerg`. The `github-pr-identity` skill enforces routing rules
per PR, but the underlying ambiguity is still there. Should the primary
identity be (a) Matt's personal GitHub long-term, (b) a Zerg-org / matteisn-zerg
account explicitly, or (c) keep dual + tighten the skill's routing rules?
entities.context:
Affects PR attribution on open-source contributions, brand visibility (matteisn
on Zerg PRs reads as personal credibility; matthew-zerg reads as company),
and the github-pr-identity skill's complexity. Memory note feedback_epoch_ml_org_account.md
flags that matteisn lacks Epoch-ML org access — relevant for Idan-org PRs.

options.label: Use personal GitHub (matteisn) as primary
options.implications:
Builds Matt's personal credibility. matteisn becomes the public "Zerg work"
identity on all PRs. Need org-level access fix for Epoch-ML PRs. Brand reads
as operator-led, not company-led.

options.label: Use matthew-zerg / explicit Zerg-org account as primary
options.implications:
Cleaner separation of personal/company identity. Less personal credibility
transfer. Needs the matthew-zerg account active + properly permissioned.
github-pr-identity skill simplifies (single routing rule).

options.label: Keep dual; tighten skill routing
options.implications:
No identity change. Just clarify rules: e.g., Epoch-ML/* → matteisn,
open-source/personal → matteisn, Zerg-org property work → matthew-zerg.
Maintains current state; lowest disruption.

unblocks: clarity on PR attribution for Idan-team review velocity

entities.title: Idan thought-piece cadence — start now or wait until after Zergboard launch?
entities.question:
exp-015 (RICE 98, "Idan bi-weekly thought-pieces") is queued in Phase 2 Cohort 1
but hasn't started. Should we kick off the cadence now (giving Idan 2-3 pieces
before Zergboard launch to build his audience early), wait until after the
Zergboard launch ships so Idan can focus on launch content first, or skip the
bi-weekly cadence and write thought-pieces as response to industry signals
rather than on a fixed schedule?
entities.context:
Memory note feedback_blog_author_routing.md: technical/research → Idan,
non-technical/launch → Matthew. Idan currently has unwritten capacity. The
Latent Space podcast guesting (BD target) wants founder credibility built up
before pitching. exp-015 is one of the Phase 2 Cohort 1 experiments — kill
date 2026-07-13. Without start, it can't conclude.

options.label: Start bi-weekly cadence immediately (first piece by 2026-05-25)
options.implications:
Idan ships 2-3 pieces before Zergboard launch on 5/19. Audience builds
early. Pairs well with `agent-intelligence` Zergboard launch framing if
that's chosen. Idan capacity split between launch content + cadence.

options.label: Hold until after Zergboard launch (start ~2026-06-02)
options.implications:
Idan focuses 100% on Zergboard launch content (announcement + technical
breakout). Cadence starts post-launch with 2 weeks of pent-up signal.
Tight bind if launch slips.

options.label: Drop bi-weekly schedule; write pieces responsively to industry signals
options.implications:
No fixed cadence. Idan writes when there's something real to say (paper
breakdown, AI launch reaction, technical hot take). Higher per-piece
quality bar; loses the discipline of regular shipping. Kills exp-015
as currently designed; replace with a different Cohort 1 experiment.

unblocks: Idan-led podcast guesting (Latent Space, AI Engineer pitches)

entities.title: Microproduct-level pricing — uniform tiers or per-product custom?
entities.question:
How should we price the individual microproducts (zboard, zchat, zcal,
zmail, zsend)? Uniform Free/$1/$9/$19 tiers (current vampire-attack
positioning) — or per-product custom tiers that reflect each
microproduct's actual value relative to its specific competitor?
entities.context:
Sourced from Zergboard WEB-25 (medium priority). Memory note
project_zstack_pricing.md anchors the vampire-attack on uniform
$1/$9/$19. Argument FOR uniform: simple to communicate, undercut
narrative is consistent across products, Bundle math is clean.
Argument FOR per-product: ZergCal has different willingness-to-pay
than ZergMail; uniform leaves money on the table for high-value
microproducts and over-prices low-value ones. The decision affects
every comparison page's pricing claim and the homepage hero.

options.label: Uniform $0/$1/$9/$19 across all microproducts (status quo)
options.implications:
Simple narrative. Vampire-attack works as one story. Bundle math
stays clean ($19 = all microproducts together). Leaves money on
the table for ZergCal/ZergMeeting; over-prices ZergMail/ZergSend.

options.label: Per-product tiers reflecting competitor + value
options.implications:
ZergMeeting = $19 (Otter is $30). ZergBoard = $9 (Linear is $14).
ZergMail = $1 (Front is $25). Etc. Each product priced against its
own market. Risk: lose the simple narrative; Bundle math gets
complex.

options.label: Three pricing bands (low / mid / high microproducts)
options.implications:
Compromise. Low-value microproducts (zmail/zsend) at $1/$5/$9.
Mid-value (zboard/zchat) at $1/$9/$19. High-value (zmeeting/
zcal-with-AI) at $1/$15/$29. Preserves vampire-attack at low end,
captures value at high end. Still simpler than full custom.

unblocks: All comparison pages (per-product comp vs uniform comp)

append.Notes: 2026-05-12 — Pricing DECIDED uniform-tiers per dec-microproduct-pricing. $0/$1/$9/$19 across all microproducts. Vampire-attack stays the single narrative. Bundle math: $19 = all microproducts.

2026-05-12 — Pricing DECIDED per-product-custom per dec-microproduct-pricing. Each microproduct priced against its own competitor. ZergMeeting = $19, ZergBoard = $9, ZergMail = $1, etc. Bundle math gets complex; comparison pages get richer.

2026-05-12 — Pricing DECIDED tiered-bands per dec-microproduct-pricing. Three pricing bands (low/mid/high microproducts). Preserves vampire-attack at low end, captures value at high end. Stripe setup needs three SKU rows per microproduct.

entities.title: Newsletter program start — bootstrap now or wait for ZergAudience?
entities.question:
Memory note project_personal_sites_newsletter_held.md flags the newsletter
program as held — Resend-direct integration is on hold pending ZergAudience
multi-tenant readiness. Phase 2 Cohort 2 includes "email newsletter issue 001"
but it can't ship without a sending pipeline. Should we (a) bootstrap on
Resend-direct now and migrate to ZergAudience later, (b) wait for ZergAudience
multi-tenant which unblocks both personal sites + Zerg, or (c) stay
blog-distribution-only (no dedicated newsletter) until Phase 3?
entities.context:
Memory notes: project_personal_sites_newsletter_held.md, feedback_email_capture_routing.md.
Welcome drip (6-step) is live and works through current Resend wiring. Audience
growth comes from blog/launch traffic that has no explicit newsletter signup
CTA today. Phase 2 Cohort 2 RICE budget is 379 across 4 experiments —
newsletter is one of them.

options.label: Bootstrap on Resend-direct now (migrate later)
Wait for ZergAudience multi-tenant ready
options.implications:
Clean architecture from day 1. Resend stays only for personal sites until
ZergAudience absorbs both. Newsletter doesn't ship in Phase 2 Cohort 2;
replace with a different RICE-scored experiment. Subscribers don't accumulate
during the gap.

options.label: Skip newsletter; rely on blog distribution + RSS only
options.implications:
No dedicated newsletter as a channel. RSS + Twitter/LinkedIn distribution
do all the lift. Lower friction; loses the most-engaged-audience leverage
that newsletters carry. Phase 3 retention motion has to lean on lifecycle
email triggered by product events (Zerglytics-driven) rather than
broadcast.

unblocks: Audience accumulation off Zergboard launch traffic

cascade.wait-zergaudience: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-11 — DECIDED wait-zergaudience per dec-newsletter-program-start. Newsletter doesn't ship in Phase 2 Cohort 2; pick a replacement experiment from the deferred slate.\"}}]

entities.rationale: ZergAudience already multi-tenant per zsend/db/schema.sql line 4; welcome_drip seed script exists; PR #319 wired contact-create hook. feedback_zstack_subsumes_external_tools.md → build into zsend, not Resend.

entities.title: Portfolio-wide pricing-page default-frame ("Most teams choose Pro") — adopt as convention?
entities.question:
Should we adopt a portfolio-wide convention of explicitly marking the
recommended tier on every Zerg-product pricing page via the same
default-frame treatment ("Most teams choose Pro" or equivalent), based on
actual per-product conversion data? This is a tactical convention WITHIN
the closed `dec-microproduct-pricing` (uniform-tiers $0/$1/$9/$19) — it
does not reopen that decision.
entities.context:
The 2026-05-30 → 2026-05-31 behavioral-sciences advise-bx dogfood loop
surveyed 7 Zerg-product surfaces (5 positioning docs + 1 live pricing page
+ 2 internal distinction docs). Two portfolio-wide findings emerged:

1. **Basic-to-Pro 9× compression** under-converts Pro at 5 of 7 surfaces
   (`price-tier-mid-bias`, Jachimowicz et al. 2019, DOI 10.1017/bpp.2018.43).
   This is a structural-pricing question and does NOT meet the existing
   `dec-microproduct-pricing` revisit triggers — surface as evidence for
   future revisit, not action today.

2. **Default-frame absence** at 6 of 7 surfaces — only the Zerglytics live
   pricing-page explicitly marks Pro as the recommended tier ("The default
   plan for any product team running their own sites"). The default-effect
   mechanism (Johnson & Goldstein 2003; Jachimowicz et al. 2019) is the
   single highest-leverage portfolio-wide pricing-page intervention. This
   IS implementable today inside uniform-tiers.

This decision asks: do we make default-frame a portfolio-wide convention
(apply to every Zerg-product pricing page) or treat per-product?

options.label: Adopt as portfolio-wide convention; every multi-tier Zerg pricing page marks Pro as default
options.implications:
Single design + copy convention applied across Zergboard, Zerglytics,
Zergmail, Zergsend, Zergchat, Zergcal, Zergmeeting, Zergwallet pricing
pages. Default-effect activated portfolio-wide. Conversion calibration
required per product (claim must be honest — see "Honesty boundary"
below). One-time design pass; downstream pages inherit.

Costs: requires per-product conversion-data verification before claim
lands. Pre-monetization products (most of the queue) can use a softer
("Our most popular plan for X teams") variant honestly until paid data
exists.

options.label: Per-product PM judgment; no portfolio mandate
options.implications:
Each product page decides independently. Zerglytics keeps its current
"default plan" framing; others can adopt or not. Slower spread,
uneven application, no portfolio compounding effect.

Cost: under-leverages a mechanism that's empirically the strongest
pricing-page intervention available.

options.label: Defer until ≥30 paid conversions exist on any single microproduct
options.implications:
Wait for the same threshold the parent dec-microproduct-pricing uses
for its revisit trigger. Risk-averse: nobody makes a claim before the
data. Cost: pre-monetization launches (Zergmail, Zergsend, Zergchat,
Zergmeeting, Zergwallet) ship without default-frame and have to
backfill it post-launch.

unblocks: All Zerg-product pricing-page designs (gives convention to inherit)
Per-product PM autonomy on tier-default decisions
Future revisit of dec-microproduct-pricing (cleaner before/after evidence

entities.title: Rebrand zergboard → zboard (and chat/cal/mail) public-facing?
entities.question:
Drop the "zerg" prefix on microproduct names for public-facing surfaces?
zergboard → zboard, zergchat → zchat, zergcal → zcal, zergmail → zmail.
Internal names + Zstack umbrella brand stay the same.
entities.context:
Sourced from Zergboard WEB-24 (high priority). The "zerg" prefix gives
every microproduct a clear family lineage but also signals "indie / built
by Zerg AI" which may or may not be the right vibe for the buyer audience.
Shorter names are punchier in ads, on slides, and as URLs (zboard.com vs
zergboard.com). Domain availability is a real variable — `zboard.com` and
`zchat.com` would need to be acquired. Counter: the Zerg parent brand
loses cohesion if every microproduct drops the prefix.

options.label: Rebrand to short forms (zboard, zchat, zcal, zmail, zsend)
options.implications:
Punchier names. Need to acquire short-name domains (~$500-50k each
depending on availability). All marketing copy + URLs + UI labels
need updating. Cleaner standalone product identities at the cost of
family-brand cohesion.

options.label: Keep zergboard / zergchat / zergcal / zergmail / zergsend
options.implications:
Status quo. Family cohesion preserved. No domain acquisition cost.
Buyer audience may read "zerg" as indie/playful which is on-brand for
Zerg AI but may not be the right register for enterprise GTM.

options.label: Public marketing uses short form; internal/docs keep zerg prefix
options.implications:
Homepage + product pages + landing copy use "Zboard." Internal docs
+ cards + skills + this vault keep "zergboard." Maintains family
cohesion in dev/skill ecosystem while presenting cleaner public
surface. Risk: cognitive split when external/internal context
crosses (e.g., onboarding docs).

cascade.rebrand-short: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-zergboard-launch.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — REBRAND DECIDED per dec-rebrand-shortname: zergboard → zboard public-facing. Launch announcement copy needs to lead with 'Zboard' (not 'Zergboard'). Domain acquisition needed for zboard.com.\"}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — REBRAND DECIDED rebrand-short. All comparison pages need rename: linear-vs-zboard.md, slack-vs-zchat.md, etc. Per-product landing pages re-titled.'}}]
cascade.keep-full: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-zergboard-launch.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Branding DECIDED keep-full per dec-rebrand-shortname. Launch announcement stays 'Zergboard.' No domain acquisition needed.\"}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — Branding DECIDED keep-full. Comparison pages keep zergboard/zergchat naming.'}}]
cascade.mixed-internal: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-zergboard-launch.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Branding DECIDED mixed-internal per dec-rebrand-shortname. Public marketing uses 'Zboard'; internal docs + skill names + this vault keep 'zergboard.' Launch copy = Zboard.\"}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-12 — Branding DECIDED mixed-internal. Public-facing copy = short forms; internal toolchain stays full names.'}}]

entities.title: North-star metric instrumentation (WAPW + QPV)
entities.question:
The two declared north-star metrics — WAPW (Weekly Active Pro Workspaces,
Zstack-side) and QPV (Qualified Pipeline Value 30d, Solutions-side) — are
both currently `not-instrumented` with `value: null`. The weekly growth-
dashboard cron prints red lines for both. Do we instrument both, just
WAPW, accept the gap and document it, or redefine the NSMs to metrics
we already measure?
entities.context:
No phase deadline drives this anymore (Matt 2026-05-12 dropped phase
gates; kept NSMs). The question is now ongoing-measurement-hygiene, not
pre-gate scramble. WAPW lands via Zerglytics (live infrastructure;
~6h work). QPV needs Stripe webhook + Solutions board sync that doesn't
exist; manual until then. Memory anchors: feedback_be_data_driven.md
(instrument the call early) + feedback_dashboard_must_drive_action.md
(dashboards without ground-truth become noise).

options.label: Instrument WAPW; defer QPV
options.implications:
WAPW via Zerglytics — workspace.pro_seat_count >= 1 AND human-driven
event in last 7d. ~6 hours of work. Ready the moment Pro workspaces exist.
QPV stays manual / tracked in Solutions board until Stripe webhook lands.
Defensible: Solutions is small N right now; manual is fine for that volume.

options.label: Instrument WAPW + QPV
options.implications:
WAPW via Zerglytics + QPV via manual CRM/pipeline tagging until Stripe
webhook lands. ~1 day of work total. Gives full measurement surface from
day one. Cost: QPV manual entry becomes a maintenance burden.

options.label: Accept the gap; document it
options.implications:
Both NSMs stay red on the dashboard. Document why ("instrumentation
deferred — product audience too small to be informative"). Lowest cost.
Reasonable if you accept the metrics aren't load-bearing until there's
a real audience to measure.

options.label: Redefine NSMs to what we already measure
options.implications:
Swap WAPW for weekly signups (instrumented) or weekly distinct paying
workspaces (Stripe-derived). Removes the gap by changing the goal.
Risk: NSM becomes whatever's easiest to measure, not what most reflects
product value. Lowest fidelity.

unblocks: first measured baselines for active experiments (exp-001/002/003)

WAPW spec: workspace.pro_seat_count >= 1 AND any human-driven event in last 7d. Lands via Zerglytics. ~1 day.'}}, {'entity': 'metrics/qpv.md', 'set': {'status': 'instrumented', 'instrumentation_target_date': '2026-06-04'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — DECIDED instrument both. QPV requires CRM/pipeline tagging — manual until Stripe webhook lands.'}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-nsm-instrumentation.md', 'set': {'status': 'in-progress'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — DECIDED instrument-both per dec-wapw-instrumentation. WAPW + QPV instrumentation due 2026-06-04.'}}]

Picks up product-side measurement. ~6 hours of work.'}}, {'entity': 'metrics/qpv.md', 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — DEFERRED per dec-wapw-instrumentation: QPV stays manual/red until Solutions Stripe pipe lands in Phase 2.'}}, {'entity': 'projects/proj-nsm-instrumentation.md', 'set': {'status': 'in-progress'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — DECIDED instrument-wapw-only per dec-wapw-instrumentation. QPV deferred to Phase 2.'}}]

Document in weekly/2026-06-04.md. NSMs still red at Phase 1 close.'}}]

Replaced by new metric entity (create separately).'}}, {'entity': 'metrics/qpv.md', 'set': {'status': 'deprecated'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — DEPRECATED per dec-wapw-instrumentation redefine-nsm. Create new metric entities for the swap (e.g. weekly signups, weekly distinct paying workspaces). Risk: NSM becomes whatever's easiest to measure, not what most reflects product value.\"}}]

entities.title: Internal GTM-hub web dashboard — deploy now or skip
entities.question:
The internal GTM-hub web dashboard (private viewer at zerg-gtm-hub.fly.dev,
basic-auth gated) is staged and ready to ship. Running web/setup_auth.sh once
generates a 24-char password, stores it in macOS Keychain, sets Fly secrets,
and deploys — ~1 minute. Should we run it now (4th surface for the hub),
defer 2 weeks until first Slack side-by-side cycle is over, or skip web view
entirely and stay on terminal + Slack + Obsidian?
entities.context:
The hub is already accessible from 3 surfaces — terminal (gtm-hub <cmd>),
Slack (gtm-hub Slack listener cron, every 5 min), and Obsidian
(Growth/README.md auto-regenerated hourly). A web view adds phone-browser
access and a future-hires viewing surface, at the cost of another surface
to maintain and a small Fly bill ($0 at idle thanks to auto-stop). Setup
script + nightly redeploy cron are written; auto-deploy cron is staged but
NOT auto-installed pending this call.

options.label: Deploy now via setup_auth.sh
options.implications:
Web view live at zerg-gtm-hub.fly.dev within 1 minute. Accessible from
phone, browser, anywhere. Password lives in Keychain (rotate anytime by
re-running setup_auth.sh). Auto-deploy cron stays disarmed until Matt
explicitly opts in. Net new ongoing cost: ~$0 (Fly auto-stops idle).

options.label: Defer 2 weeks; re-evaluate after first Monday side-by-side
options.implications:
Wait until Monday May 18's gtm-hub-post vs growth-dashboard side-by-side
shows whether a 4th surface is actually wanted. Decide after seeing real
usage. Cost: zero; staged files stay parked, ~1 min to deploy when ready.

options.label: Skip web view entirely
options.implications:
Terminal + Slack + Obsidian cover the operator-Matt use case. Drop the
Phase 4 web buildout. Optionally remove the staged web/ files. Cost: minor
cleanup; reduces surface count which means less drift risk.

unblocks: phone / browser access to the hub
external read-only viewing surface (future Idan + hires)

entities.title: Welcome drip sign-off — "Matthew + the Zerg Team" or "— Matt"?
entities.question:
Welcome drip emails currently sign off "Matthew + the Zerg Team" — formal
team-voice. Switch to "— Matt" (closer, founder-style) or keep the
current team-voice signoff?
entities.context:
Sourced from Zergboard MAR-31 ([Decision] low priority). The welcome drip
is the first email contact new signups have with Zerg. "Matthew + the
Zerg Team" signals "real company, real team." "— Matt" signals "small
team, you'll talk to the founder." Both work; question is which matches
the brand Matt wants new signups to see. Memory note feedback_email_reply_voice.md
uses "Matt" in personal-catch-up emails; professional outbound uses
"Matthew Eisner" per feedback_matt_full_name.md.

options.label: Keep "Matthew + the Zerg Team"
options.implications:
Status quo. Signals legitimacy + team operation. Defensible if the
product is meant to feel enterprise-credible from email #1.

options.label: Switch to "— Matt"
options.implications:
Founder-style closeness. Signals "small team, reply if you want."
Higher likelihood of replies (people reply to humans). Risk: signals
smaller-than-reality team to enterprise prospects.

options.label: Tier by audience — "— Matt" for Pro/Free, "Matthew + the Zerg Team" for Bundle/Enterprise
options.implications:
Both. Pro/Free signups get the founder-personal touch; Bundle/Enterprise
get the team-voice. Requires drip routing on signup tier — small
engineering lift in email-drip skill. Probably overkill.

unblocks: All Phase 2 nurture sequences (inherits the same voice)

cascade.keep-team: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Drip signoff DECIDED keep-team per dec-welcome-drip-signoff. Welcome drip stays 'Matthew + the Zerg Team.' Phase 2 nurture sequences inherit the team-voice.\"}}]
cascade.switch-matt: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Drip signoff DECIDED switch-matt per dec-welcome-drip-signoff. Welcome drip switches to '— Matt' founder-voice. Phase 2 nurture sequences match.\"}}]
cascade.hybrid-tier: [{'entity': 'projects/proj-content-pipeline.md', 'append': {'Notes': \"2026-05-12 — Drip signoff DECIDED hybrid-tier per dec-welcome-drip-signoff. Pro/Free signups get '— Matt'; Bundle/Enterprise get 'Matthew + the Zerg Team.' email-drip skill needs signup-tier routing logic.\"}}]

entities.rationale: Drip draft at MattZerg/Writing/Zergboard Welcome Drip.md line 39 already specifies '— Matt'. Body voice matches. feedback_matt_full_name.md is blog-byline-scoped. fakeidan-verified.

entities.title: Zergboard public launch framing
entities.question:
When Zergboard ships publicly (date NOT confirmed; aspirational target was
May 19 but per 2026-05-12 Matt directive all launches are ship-when-ready),
should the campaign lead with product framing ($1/seat vampire-attack pricing
against Linear/Asana), the broader Agent Intelligence thesis (Zergboard as
visible artifact of an agent-native operations platform), or hold until a
signed-off customer signal lands first? Blog cadence runs independently of
launch readiness — this decision shapes framing when the launch IS ready,
not when to launch.
entities.context:
The launch announcement is drafted in vault (Writing/Launches/). Vault memory
feedback_zergboard_launch_unknown.md flags the plan as not-confirmed; memory
feedback_reconfirm_launch_plans.md requires re-confirming before acting. The
framing choice cascades into hero imagery, X/LinkedIn copy, the MAR-15
Marketing card, and which BD targets are pitched first. Current content
entity content/zergboard-launch.md has target_date 2026-05-19 and status=drafted.

options.label: Lead with Zergboard (product-first, $1/seat wedge)
options.implications:
Vampire-attack pricing as the headline. Direct comparison vs Linear/Asana/Trello.
Audience: PMs, project leads. Cleaner buyer pattern, narrower TAM. Pairs with
pSEO comparison pages already drafted. Lower differentiation vs incumbents.

options.label: Lead with Agent Intelligence (thesis-first)
options.implications:
Position Zergboard as the visible artifact of an "agent-native operations"
thesis. Audience: AI builders / forward operators. Higher differentiation;
less specific buyer. Pairs with Idan thought-pieces; requires the broader
positioning brief to land first.

options.label: Defer launch 4 weeks (push to June 16)
options.implications:
Use the time to (a) get a customer signal we can quote, (b) finish 1-2 more
Andesite/CesiumAstro case studies, (c) pick framing based on what's actually
resonating. Trades immediate activation for higher-quality launch.

unblocks: MAR-15 Zergboard public launch sequence (Marketing board)

cascade.zergboard: [{'entity': 'content/zergboard-launch.md', 'set': {'framing_lane': 'product-first'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — Framing decided: Zergboard product-first ($1/seat wedge). Hero, social, BD targets all lean to PM/project-lead audience.'}}, {'entity': 'launches/zergboard-public-launch.md', 'set': {'framing': 'product-first'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — Framing: product-first per dec-zergboard-launch-framing.'}}, {'entity': 'proj-zergboard-launch', 'set': {'status': 'in-progress'}}]
cascade.agent-intelligence: [{'entity': 'content/zergboard-launch.md', 'set': {'framing_lane': 'thesis-first'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — Framing decided: Agent Intelligence thesis-first. Zergboard as visible artifact; audience = AI builders / forward operators.'}}, {'entity': 'launches/zergboard-public-launch.md', 'set': {'framing': 'thesis-first'}, 'append': {'Notes': '2026-05-11 — Framing: thesis-first per dec-zergboard-launch-framing.'}}, {'entity': 'proj-zergboard-launch', 'set': {'status': 'in-progress'}}]

New target 2026-06-16. Use the time for customer signal + case-study captures.'}}, {'entity': 'launches/zergboard-public-launch.md', 'set': {'ship_date': '2026-06-16', 'status': 'drafting'}}]

entities.title: ZergMeeting — Zstack umbrella or standalone product?
entities.question:
Should ZergMeeting (the meeting transcription / agent-attendable meeting
product) launch under the Zstack umbrella alongside Zergboard/ZergChat/
ZergCal — or as a standalone product with its own brand?
entities.context:
Sourced from Zergboard MAR-25 ([Launches] high priority). ZergMeeting is
the only Zstack microproduct whose buyer ICP differs meaningfully from
the others (call leaders, sales teams, conference attendees) vs the
workspace-tool ICP for board/chat/cal/mail. Standalone would let it
pursue a focused GTM (Otter killer); umbrella keeps it as part of the
AI-native suite. Affects the Phase 2 cohort planning + the comparison
page strategy (one Otter comp page vs a generic suite comp page).

options.label: Launch under Zstack umbrella (consistent w/ other microproducts)
options.implications:
Same brand family, same pricing tier structure, same homepage
placement. Bundle includes ZergMeeting. Marketing carries it as
one-of-six. Loses Otter-killer focus; gains family cohesion.

options.label: Standalone product brand + GTM
options.implications:
New brand (e.g., Zinger, Recap, Verbatim). Independent landing page
+ comparison vs Otter/Fathom/Fireflies. NOT in the Bundle. Focused
pitch for sales-team buyers. Higher GTM cost; more freedom.

options.label: Zstack-branded but with its own GTM track
options.implications:
Still "ZergMeeting" + family pricing. But independent landing page,
independent Phase 2 launch sequence, dedicated Otter-comparison
content. Hedges between focus and family cohesion.

unblocks: Phase 2 cohort planning (which microproduct launches when)

append.Notes: 2026-05-12 — ZergMeeting DECIDED umbrella per dec-zergmeeting-umbrella. Same brand family + Zstack pricing tier + Bundle inclusion. Marketing carries it as one-of-six microproducts.
2026-05-12 — ZergMeeting DECIDED standalone per dec-zergmeeting-umbrella. New brand + independent landing page + comparison content. NOT in the Bundle. Focused Otter-killer GTM. Higher cost.
2026-05-12 — ZergMeeting DECIDED hybrid per dec-zergmeeting-umbrella. Zstack-branded + family pricing, but independent landing page + Phase 2 launch sequence + dedicated Otter comparison content.

entities.title: Agent Ops Assessment Tool — "Is your agent rollout ready?"
Product BD program — integration / co-marketing / podcast / ecosystem
entities.status_summary:
28 named BD targets across 4 categories (integration 5, co-marketing 8, podcast
8, ecosystem 7). Only Anthropic (Claude Code / MCP) is in active outreach —
MCP listing submitted 2026-05-06. The other 27 are `planned`. Phase 2 target
is 2 integration + 2 co-marketing partnerships closed by 2026-08-04.

Acquired / 20VC held for funding milestone (no near-term action)

entities.title: Case study flywheel — 4 published in 90 days
entities.status_summary:
3 case studies in capture: Andesite, CesiumAstro, Durable. All status=capture,
NDA unverified, customer signoff pending. Andesite draft 1320 words. Phase 2
Day 90 target: 4 case studies published. Without signoff pace, this becomes

Customer-side signoff loop unresolved on each

Next near-term ship: "Most AI Agents Have No Memory" (status=scheduled,

Zerglytics, Zstack Bundle). Healthy pipeline shape; distribution coverage
is the gap (3/17 distributed).

entities.title: North-star metric instrumentation (WAPW + QPV)
entities.status_summary:
